justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth. Formal escalation produced with explicit call for executive arbitration. No acceptance criteria produced (array is empty). confidence.level is 40, within the <=45 threshold. Compromise options (abstracted proximity, ETA-based tracking, driver-consented sharing) are clearly labeled as proposals/options, not resolutions.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder characterization — goes beyond the two named stakeholders to identify Legal/Compliance as a critical missing stakeholder
Empty business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays correctly reflect that nothing can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Formal escalation with structured options (A/B/C/D) and a recommended path, all clearly framed as proposals requiring executive decision
rejection_reasons array explicitly states the spec is unimplementable as written — strong signal that Bird is not pretending the conflict is resolved
Edge cases are well-chosen and each notes that behavior cannot be defined without resolving the core contradiction
Identifies the false equivalence between 'customer transparency' and 'exact GPS' as a root cause of the conflict — sophisticated domain analysis
Risk analysis correctly identifies asymmetry: losing drivers is harder to recover from than missing a feature, showing genuine business reasoning rather than false balance

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands no location sharing to protect driver retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver
